figures.caption: Table 2: Multi-hop tasks and execution statistics for agent runs and participants.

Fig. 4: Macro-averaged transition graphs by cohort (TE set). Node size: visitation share; edge width: transition frequency. For readability, the participant-only track node is omitted from the plots but included in all metrics. Shown are transitions with probability ≥ 0.05; transitions with < 0.1 probability are in gray.

Fig. 3: Coverage of agent queries within the participants’ query space. Curves compare agent (green), a size-matched random subset of unique participant queries (pink; mean with 95% bootstrap CI shaded), and a top-N frequency oracle (gold). (a) N fixed to the total number of unique agent queries (N = 40). (b) Each curve adds k queries, with k varying up to Nmax = 40. Both panels use the full query set (TE).
Fig. 2: Prompt instructions added to the AppAgent system prompt to encourage in-application reasoning rather than answering from prior knowledge.

Fig. 1: Overview of the trace-level evaluation framework: task design, human–agent trace collection in a production search application, and behavioral comparison across outcomes, query formulation, and navigation metrics.

Table 3: Task success and effort by cohort (CS Set; all tasks). Success is reported as completed tasks (s/n), percentage, and 95% CI. Time is mean ± SD (s); actions are median [Q1, Q3]. Statistical comparisons are reported in the text.
